summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend e2e testing of SynkData Identity Intelligence Platform MVP. 25/25 backend pytest tests passed (auth, identity validators, sanctions, master /api/checks with AI report, analytics, digital). Frontend: landing, login (pre-filled admin), dashboard with all stat cards, CURP/RFC/Sanctions validator pages, History list, Check Results page (3 gauges + cytoscape graph + RENAPO/SAT/IMSS/RND cards + AI report), 4-step wizard with proper validation gating, logout — all working. Found 2 product issues: clipboard copy in API Docs throws uncaught error, and history rows are only clickable via name link (not entire row).

minor.issue: Default threshold=85 does NOT match 'Joaquin Guzman Loera' (El Chapo) — only matches at threshold<=80. Frontend UI default 85 will therefore return no matches for this seeded entry. Consider lowering default threshold in services/sanctions.py screen_sanctions() to 80, or improving the matched names in SANCTIONS_DB to score higher with thefuzz.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText fails with NotAllowedError in iframe/preview context and the error propagates as an unhandled runtime error producing a full-screen 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay (react-error-overlay). This overlay then blocks ALL subsequent clicks on the page (sidebar nav, logout) until the user dismisses it. Wrap clipboard.writeText in try/catch and show a toast on failure.

Only the subject name <Link> is clickable (data-testid='link-<id>'). Clicking elsewhere on a row (TR with data-testid='row-<id>') does NOT navigate to /app/checks/{id}. Spec says 'clicking a row navigates to results'. Either make the entire <tr> clickable (onClick + cursor-pointer) or document that only the name link navigates.

issues: Default UMBRAL DE MATCH=85 misses the 'El Chapo' OFAC entry (matches at 80). New users searching the most famous test case will see 'SIN COINCIDENCIAS' which is confusing. Lower default to 80 or surface a hint.

action_items: Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch in ApiDocs.jsx so a denied clipboard does NOT bubble as an unhandled error/overlay that blocks UI
Make entire history table <tr> clickable (or add onClick navigation) so 'clicking a row navigates to results' as per spec
Lower default sanctions threshold from 85 to 80 (or add a 'Joaquin' alias to SANCTIONS_DB) so seeded test case El Chapo triggers at default settings
Consider adding a small note on Sanctions UI explaining what threshold means and recommended values
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 457 lines and starting to mix concerns (auth, identity, government, sanctions, digital, checks, analytics) — split into routers/* modules before it grows past 700 lines

POST /api/checks runs all modules synchronously in a single request; AI report alone can take 5-15s. Consider returning the check immediately and generating ai_report in a background task, then polling/streaming the report
Frontend: ApiDocs copy uses navigator.clipboard.writeText without try/catch — anti-pattern, always handle clipboard rejection
Sanctions UI default threshold (85) is too strict for the seeded fuzzy matches; misaligned with backend defaults causes inconsistent test behavior across modules
test_credentials: admin@synkdata.mx / Admin2026! and analyst@synkdata.mx / Analyst2026! — verified working
seed_data_creation: Created 1 background check for Juan Carlos Lopez Hernandez via /api/checks during testing (used as fixture). Also registered a few transient TEST_*@synkdata.mx users via /auth/register.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_synkdata.py covers auth, identity, sanctions (with threshold=80 explicit for El Chapo), master /api/checks (validates AI report >500 chars), analytics, digital. To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_synkdata.py -v. Two known frontend issues: (1) clipboard copy overlay (ApiDocs.jsx) and (2) history row click only via the link. After fixes, retest those two flows. Sanctions default threshold mismatch (85 vs 80) is a UX/config decision for main agent.

rca_of_the_issue.clipboard_overlay: ApiDocs.jsx calls navigator.clipboard.writeText() without await/catch. In preview iframe + cross-origin contexts the call rejects with NotAllowedError. The unhandled promise rejection is caught by React's dev error overlay (react-error-overlay), which renders a full-screen modal that intercepts pointer events. As a result, the next nav click (e.g., nav-new-check, logout-btn) happens 'on the overlay' and never reaches the real button. Fix: wrap in try/catch and show a UI toast, OR detect clipboard permission first.
rca_of_the_issue.history_row_click: History.jsx only attaches navigation to the inner <Link> (data-testid='link-<id>'); the <tr data-testid='row-<id>'> has only a hover style — no onClick. Spec implies whole-row clickable. Fix: add onClick={() => navigate(`/app/checks/${c.id}`)} on the <tr> with cursor-pointer styling, OR widen the Link to cover all cells.
